title: WCAG 2.3.3 Animation from Interactions
summary: WCAG requires a mechanism to pause, stop, or hide motion animations triggered by user interaction, with prefers-reduced-motion as the standard method.
supportingClaims: Non-essential animations must respect user motion preferences via the prefers-reduced-motion media query.
Vestibular disorders affect an estimated 15-20% of the population, making motion reduction an accessibility requirement.
